text:
Solid take, Hunter...  $ALMU is flying under the radar. 15+ years in semis and interconnects here: GPUs steal the show, but moving massive data at scale is the real bottleneck — power, heat, and copper limits are killing efficiency. 

Aeluma’s III-V-on-silicon photonics on standard 200/300mm wafers could slash costs and scale like crazy vs. old InP tech. Strong partners (Tower, AIM, gov contracts) and real tech edge.

Early revenue, high execution risk, but at ~$414M cap, the upside in the AI optics wave is huge. 

Watch this one.Great thread. Adding to the list.(DYOR — small caps = high risk!)

text:
A team at Westlake University in China just published a handheld cancer detector in Nature Photonics. Single drop of blood, 94.9% accuracy for early-stage lung cancer, 10,000× more sensitive than the standard lab test.

Chip cost: $5.Worth paying attention to, but also worth reading carefully. That 94.9% comes from 171 patient samples. The device replaces refrigerator-sized lab equipment with an LED, a photodetector, and a 3D metamaterial chip. Genuinely impressive physics.

However, the gap nobody's talking about: every year produces 3-4 "revolutionary cancer detection" papers. Almost none survive the jump from Nature paper to clinical validation at scale.

Caveat here: 171 patients is a starting point, not proof. But the engineering approach is what's worth watching.

text: Massive gains often trigger regulatory scrutiny—expected given the timing of the Nasdaq dual-listing news. A "lottery ticket" position is one thing, but chasing this at 30x is a different game entirely.

text:
I just hit my first 10-bagger (+943%) on $SIVE in under 3 months! Discovered via @aleabitoreddit , a fundamental deep dive showed massive undervaluation in the photonics sector.

I initially bought 900 shares at 8.2 SEK, but the stock took off so fast I only managed to add a bit more below 20 SEK. The biggest lesson this investment taught me: trust your analysis and build a heavier position sooner when the conviction is there.

I locked in profits by trimming 1/3 at 49 SEK. With the price now around 85.55 SEK, I won't be adding at current valuations, but I am happily letting the rest ride as long as the company keeps delivering. Great investment, invaluable lessons.

text:
The market has figured out photonics. It’s catching on to power semis. But It still hasn’t woken up to fiber. 

The real chokepoint is sitting in the dirt. I'm positioned accordingly in $HLIT.

“Global fiber prices have risen ~70% from a 2021 trough of $3.70 to ~$6.30 per fiber-kilometer.”
 — Tom’s Hardware

“North American demand growth is projected at 22% to 25% this year, while supply expansion trails at 12% to 19%.”  
— Rebio Group estimates, reported by Tom’s Hardware

“That dual demand [AI + BEAD] elevates our fiber forecast, but it also makes the shortage more pronounced.”  
— Anis Khemakhem, Clearfield

“AI-focused data centers require significantly more fiber — about 36x more fiber than traditional CPU-based racks — to handle the massive data volumes and high-speed connectivity required by GPU clusters.”  
— Rahul Puri, CEO Optical Networking, STL

“Data center fiber demand grew roughly 76% year-on-year in 2025… the segment is projected to account for 30% of total global fiber demand by 2027. In 2024, that figure was below 5%.”
 — CRU data, reported by Tom’s Hardware

“AI has pushed fiber demand to levels we’ve never seen to ensure the incredible speeds inside data centers and between campuses. The many, massive data center builds we’re seeing today are driving huge volumes of intra-bay, inter-bay and middle-mile fiber.”
 — Anis Khemakhem, CCO, Clearfield

“The surge in hyperscale and AI network loads has significantly increased our expectations for fiber demand. Enterprise sales grew 58% year-over-year in Q3 2025, driven by continued strong adoption of Corning’s Gen AI products.”
 — John McGirr, Corning

“The U.S. will need to add 213.3 million more fiber miles by 2029, more than doubling its current amount from 159.6 million fiber miles to 372.9 million miles.”
 — Rahul Puri, STL

Major beneficiaries include:
$HLIT, $NOK, $LUMN, $CXDO, $CLFD, $BDC, $GLW

If AST SpaceMobile is the company building the connectivity layer from space, $HLIT (Harmonic) is its terrestrial mirror. The platform layer powers every fiber and cable network on the ground. Companies that build real technical moats inside secular spending waves, and get ignored while doing it, don’t stay ignored forever. AST taught me what that looks like before the re-rate. I’m seeing it again with Harmonic. And the timing matters. We’re at the front end of a global fiber supercycle, operators everywhere are being forced to densify, upgrade to DOCSIS 4.0, and push fiber deeper into their networks simultaneously. That spending wave is just starting to inflect, and Harmonic cOS is the platform sitting at the center of it. Companies that build real technical moats inside secular spending waves, and get ignored while doing it, don’t stay ignored forever. AST taught me what that looks like before the re-rate. I’m seeing it again with Harmonic. The operator accumulation flywheel is accelerating for Harmonic. Rest-of-market revenue surged 78% YoY in Q1, now 42% of total, up from near-zero a few years ago. New wins are landing every quarter across regional ISPs, international carriers, and fiber-first builders. This is the same pattern AST followed: anchor with tier-1 partners, prove the platform, then watch adoption compound as word travels through the operator community. Harmonic engineered a defined platform that creates a true moat legacy hardware vendors simply cannot replicate: • cOS™ — the industry’s first virtualized CMTS, replacing expensive proprietary hardware boxes with software running on generic servers • Unified DOCSIS + Fiber — a single stack managing both cable and fiber subscribers, the only platform that does this at scale • 150+ operators, 45.7M devices deployed — deep operational stickiness in billing, provisioning, and field workflows • 75% power reduction across seven successive hardware generations, iterative engineering lead nearly a decade in the making • AI-driven network intelligence, an entirely new growth vector built on top of the virtualized core This is full-stack vertical integration done right. The result? A company shedding its legacy video business ($145M divestiture closing Q2 2026) to emerge as a pure-play broadband software intelligence platform. Q1 2026 broadband revenue surged 43% YoY. Rest-of-market revenue, the diversification engine, grew 78%. Record $582M backlog, up 87% YoY. Management raised full-year guidance to $475–495M. text:
COMPUTING IS BECOMING THE “NEW OIL” 

The first AI trade was hardware: #chips, #memory, #photonics.

But none of that matters without live compute capacity.

That is why #NeoClouds matter.

I see the sector in 4 layers:

Tier 1: full-stack leaders  
Power + data centers + GPUs + software + enterprise customers.  

Best quality, but already crowded and priced. $NBIS is the cleanest winner.

Tier 2: power-secured AI infra operators  
Real power + data centers + GPUs + growing platform/software layer.  

Less crowded, still catching up, already monetizing. 

This is where I place $DGXX.

Tier 3: pure GPU rental  
Good cycle trade, but less structural.

Tier 4: power / land optionality  
Big upside, but still earlier in conversion.

To me, Tier 2 is the best alpha zone:

- not fully priced like Tier 1,  
- more real than Tier 3,  
- further along than Tier 4.

That is why $DGXX still looks under-discovered to me. 

Added more during dip today, will HOLD for long term investment. 

Risk/reward details are in my previous tweet.

text:
AI photonics continues gaining momentum as data centers search for faster and more efficient optical interconnect technologies.

POET Technologies has secured $400 million in financing to help expand manufacturing infrastructure, scale R&D, accelerate its light source business, and support growing demand from AI and data center markets. The company is focused on photonic integrated circuits, optical engines, and optical interconnect technologies designed for next-generation AI infrastructure. 

The funding comes as POET and Lumilens also advance wafer-level photonic integration technologies aimed at improving bandwidth density, power efficiency, and scalability for future AI optical networks. 

A fascinating look at how photonics manufacturing and optical interconnect technologies are scaling alongside AI infrastructure growth.

Read the full article here:
https://t.co/TJxRVrIqhy

text:
Pour $SOI le marché price déjà le scénario photonics 2028 de Morgan Stanley dans le cours actuel. Tu paies aujourd'hui environ ~25x EV/EBITDA pour une boîte en perte nette au H1. le marché price déjà le meilleur scénario, donc le risk/reward est symétrique.  

Si l'adoption de la photonique est rapide brutale, et que le bottleneck se matérialise elle grimpera encore. 

Mais si au contraire il y'a le moindre retard, que les industriels sont sceptiques parce que la photonique est trop chère et privilégies d'autres alternatives (comme l'ultra densification par ex) $SOI est déjà trop chère.

Kalray c'est l'IP propriétaire (MPPA) sur un marché DPU en pleine structuration. avec un modèle qui passe au licensing/co-développement (léger en capital), et un catalyseur binaire imminent (OpenChip peut prendre 51% d'ici juillet 2026). à €127M de cap, le marché ne price pas grand chose et reste toujours prduent. 
Mais ça reste un pari également, juste moins fortement valorisé à ce stade.

text:
$SCIA looks interesting again after a breakout quarter. Trading at single digit run-rate EPS w record backlog, no debt and 99%+ of revs tied to the photonics industry.

SCIA's sputtering targets (mainly ITO) show up in dozens of peer-reviewed studies next to Coherent's excimer lasers. Acc to an industry expert, Coherent sources ~40% of its materials externally.

Mgmt attributed a big part of the breakout quarter to new products launched last year. They introduced two, but they consistently flag one: a new ITO formulation.

text:
As data center demands accelerate, the future of AI will depend not only on processing power, but on how efficiently systems are connected.

@robpegoraro  explores how photonics can address the growing pressure for AI data centers, and what role optical networking plays in the connectivity stack of the AI infrastructure.

Read more on @PCMag , featuring Taara CEO Mahesh Krishnaswamy, NTT, and @nttdata_inc's #IOWN: 

https://t.co/V4xtkT2KoD
@crux_capital_ I agree, but my main worry with photonics is that its pullback isn’t necessarily a new base. It’s trading back to where it was pre ATH
